# Helen W. Leung PhD

## Identity
Name: Helen W. Leung PhD
Status: Open to roles in life sciences strategy, investment, and scientific advisory
Location: London, UK
Nationality: British-Chinese
Languages: English, Mandarin Chinese, Cantonese
Portfolio: https://helenwl.github.io/mywork

## Skills
Scientific: 3D Electron Diffraction (3D-ED / MicroED), 4D-STEM, 4D Scanning Electron Diffraction (4D-SED), Synchrotron X-ray Powder Diffraction (XRPD), Rietveld Refinement, Pharmaceutical Crystallography, Crystal Structure Determination, Polymorph Characterisation, Cryo-EM
Computational: Crystal Structure Prediction, Convolutional Neural Networks, ML anomaly detection
Commercial: Pharmaceutical Strategy, Commercial Due Diligence, Vendor Due Diligence, M&A Due Diligence
Domains: Small Molecule Drug Discovery, Long-Acting Injectables, Nanoscience, Drug Manufacturing, Health Equity, FemTech, Precision Medicine, China–Europe Biotech

## Experience
- L.E.K. Consulting | Life Sciences Specialist | London | current
  - Pharmaceutical strategy, commercial and vendor due diligence
  - Focus: new drug modalities, medical manufacturing, China–Europe biotech
- LCFI (Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence, Cambridge) | Student Fellow | pro bono
  - Hosted Cambridge Tech Week founder events (most recent: 2024)
- GSK | Medicines Supply and Development PhD Researcher | industrial PhD sponsor
  - Nano-milling of cabotegravir (long-acting injectable HIV drug), alongside PhD
- Altrove (EF-backed deep-tech startup) | AI Researcher: Crystal Structure Prediction
  - Crystal structure prediction pipeline for rare-earth mineral production
- Morgan Stanley | Trading Business and Data Analyst | European Equities Trades Technology
  - Analysis of trading flow through London Stock Exchange; ML anomaly detection model

## Projects
- Sex-specific drug dosage prediction: AI tool built from clinical trial data (LCFI)
- Virtual lab: pipeline connecting experimental outputs to in-house AI model (Altrove)
- 5 small molecule crystal structures solved during PhD (Cambridge / GSK)
- 4 first-author peer-reviewed publications (* = first author)

## Education
- University of Cambridge | PhD, Materials Science & Metallurgy, Electron Microscopy Group | GSK-sponsored | 2021–2025
  - Supervisor: Prof. Paul Midgley
- University of Oxford | MEng Materials Science, First Class | 2017–2021
  - Specialisation: analytical techniques in semiconductors for transistors
  - Final-year project: CNNs for electron micrograph analysis (Prof. Angus Kirkland)

## Publications
- Leung HW* et al. "Polytypes and planar defects revealed in the purine base xanthine using multi-dimensional electron diffraction." Communications Chemistry (Nature Portfolio), 2025. https://www.nature.com/articles/s42004-025-01729-2
- Leung HW* et al. "Revealing the crystal structure of the purine base xanthine with three-dimensional (3D) electron diffraction." Crystal Growth & Design (ACS), 2025. https://pubs.acs.org/doi/full/10.1021/acs.cgd.4c01594
- Leung HW* et al. "From formulation to structure: 3D electron diffraction for the structure solution of a new indomethacin polymorph from an amorphous solid dispersion." IUCrJ, 2024. https://journals.iucr.org/m/issues/2024/05/00/of5005/index.html
- Leung HW* et al. Electron diffraction review across pharmaceutical pipeline — submitted 2024 # TODO: add journal and DOI when published

## Target Roles
- Life sciences commercial strategy (pharma / biotech)
- Scientific due diligence for M&A and investment
- Life sciences VC/PE investment
- Advisory bridging deep scientific expertise and commercial strategy
- China–Europe cross-border biotech roles
